title: Stenarrhena lanata

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stenarrhena lanata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stenarrhena lanata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin infections.

This family includes over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The genus Stenarrhena, to which Stenarrhena lanata belongs, is a relatively small group of plants that are native to Africa and Madagascar. The species Stenarrhena lanata is likely to be further classified within the genus, but this information is not readily available. Further research into the specific classification of Stenarrhena lanata may be necessary to determine its full classification.
